findings.description: The source provides two concrete examples explaining the decision rationale for Entity vs. direct Form property, both omitted. (1) 内線番号→UsersEntity illustrates table-mapped properties going through Entity. (2) 新パスワード・確認用パスワード→direct Form property explains that because the DB stores only an encrypted password (no plain-text column), these cannot map to an Entity and must be added directly. The encryption rationale is a non-obvious design constraint that is decision-relevant.
findings.source_evidence: 後者の例を挙げると、本サンプルで画面から入力する、"新(変更後)パスワード"と"パスワード(確認用)"は2つが平文で入力されるが、システムアカウントテーブルには暗号化されたパスワードを格納するカラムだけがある。この場合、これらのプロパティは、直接Formクラスのプロパティとして追加する。

findings.description: The PascalCase class name `UsersEntity` appears in the s5 code example (field declaration and @ValidationTarget setter) but is absent from s5's hints array. Per V4, PascalCase class names from content should be included in hints.
findings.source_evidence: private UsersEntity users; ... @ValidationTarget public void setUsers(UsersEntity users) { this.users = users; }
